I have all the facts. Here is my report, organized by the 7 points.

## 1. How yazi is installed

**File:** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`

yazi is installed via **scoop**, in the "Core CLI toolset — the shell baseline, always installed" block (lines 137–152). There is **no install toggle** — yazi is unconditional (it's in the always-installed baseline, not gated behind any `installYazi`/`if` block):

```powershell
# Core CLI toolset — the shell baseline, always installed.
Scoop-Install @(
    'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', 'make', 'just',
    'starship', 'zoxide', 'fzf', 'fd', 'ripgrep', 'bat', 'eza', 'jq',
    'delta', 'lazygit', 'neovim', 'yazi', 'btop', 'television', 'tlrc',
    'uv', 'gnupg',
    ...
```

`Scoop-Install` (lines 77–93) diffs against `scoop export` and only installs missing apps. Not npm, not winget.

## 2. How Git for Windows is installed

**File:** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`, line 139 (same core scoop list as yazi):

```powershell
Scoop-Install @(
    'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', ...
```

Git is installed via **scoop**, not winget. This is significant for your fix: scoop's git lives at `~/scoop/apps/git/current/`, so `file.exe` is at **`~/scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe`** — NOT the winget `C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe` path you mentioned. (There is no winget `Git.Git` line anywhere in the script.) Note scoop typically also exposes a `current` symlink and shims under `~/scoop/shims`, but the MSYS `file.exe` is under `usr/bin`, which scoop does not shim.

## 3. The yazi PowerShell profile fragment

**File:** `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/35_yazi.ps1` (full contents):

```powershell
# 35_yazi.ps1 — `y` launches yazi and cd's to wherever you quit (mirrors the
# unix y() wrapper). No-op if yazi isn't installed.
if (Get-Command yazi -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
    function y {
        $tmp = New-TemporaryFile
        try {
            yazi @args --cwd-file $tmp.FullName
            $cwd = (Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim()
            if ($cwd -and $cwd -ne $PWD.Path -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cwd)) {
                Set-Location -LiteralPath $cwd
            }
        } finally {
            Remove-Item -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        }
    }
}
```

This fragment sets **no env vars** — it only defines the `y` shell-wrapper function (guarded by a `Get-Command yazi` presence check). `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` is NOT set here; it's set in `00_env.ps1` (see point 4). There is no `YAZI_FILE_ONE` here.

## 4. The env / environment-variable profile fragment

**File:** `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1` (full contents):

```powershell
# 00_env.ps1 — environment variables & PATH. Sourced first by $PROFILE.

# UTF-8 console I/O. ...
try {
    $utf8 = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
    [Console]::OutputEncoding = $utf8
    $OutputEncoding = $utf8
} catch { $null = $_ }

# XDG-style base dirs. ...
if (-not $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME) { $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $HOME '.config' }
if (-not $env:XDG_DATA_HOME)   { $env:XDG_DATA_HOME   = Join-Path $HOME '.local/share' }
if (-not $env:XDG_STATE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_STATE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.local/state' }
if (-not $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.cache' }

$env:STARSHIP_CONFIG = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'starship.toml'
# yazi looks in %APPDATA%\yazi\config by default; keep it XDG-consistent instead.
$env:YAZI_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'yazi'

# uv-managed Python: ...
$env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'

# Default editor
if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $env:EDITOR = 'nvim' }

# Prepend user bin dirs to PATH, idempotently. ...
$UserPaths = @(
    (Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'),
    (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/shims')
) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } |
    ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($_) } | Select-Object -Unique

$SepPaths = $env:PATH -split ';'
foreach ($p in $UserPaths) {
    if ($SepPaths -notcontains $p) { $env:PATH = "$p;$env:PATH" }
}
```

**Idioms used here:**
- Session env vars are set with `$env:FOO = ...`, guarded by `if (-not $env:FOO)` for the XDG dirs (so a pre-existing value wins), and unconditionally for derived ones like `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME`.
- **`YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` is set here** (line 22) to `$env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME/yazi` (= `~/.config/yazi`).
- PATH is prepended idempotently by splitting on `;` and only adding entries not already present.

**Related file — persistent (User-scope) env vars:** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_03_xdg_env.ps1`. This is the counterpart that persists the same XDG vars to the **User environment** (so tools launched outside a pwsh session that sourced the profile still honor `~/.config`). It uses the `[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable($name, $value, 'User')` idiom (lines 25–35), writing only when the value differs. Note: it persists only the four `XDG_*` vars — it does NOT persist `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` (that one is session-only via `00_env.ps1`).

- `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` → `~/.config/yazi` (set in `00_env.ps1` line 22, derived from `XDG_CONFIG_HOME`).

- **Only one file present:** `dot_config/yazi/yazi.toml` (there is no `keymap.toml`, `theme.toml`, `init.lua`, or `package.toml`). Full contents:

```toml
# ~/.config/yazi/yazi.toml (Windows). Resolved via $YAZI_CONFIG_HOME, set in
# profile.d/00_env.ps1. ...
# yazi 25/26 uses the [mgr] table (older versions used [manager]).

[mgr]
ratio = [1, 4, 3]
sort_by = "natural"
sort_dir_first = true
linemode = "size"
show_hidden = true
show_symlink = true

[open]
rules = [
  { url = "*/", use = ["edit", "git", "reveal", "open"] },
  { mime = "text/*", use = ["edit", "open"] },
  { mime = "*", use = ["open"] },
]

[opener]
git = [
  { run = 'cmd /c cd /d "%*" && lazygit', block = true, desc = "Lazygit (here)" },
]
```

The default (unset) location would be `%APPDATA%\yazi\config`, but the comment/env explicitly redirect it to the XDG path.

44	    # $true when this pwsh is running elevated (member of the Administrators role).

51	function Ensure-Scoop {
52	    if (-not (Have scoop)) {
53	        # get.scoop.sh returns install.ps1's text. Piping it to Invoke-Expression
54	        # works in a normal console, but an ELEVATED one is refused ("Running the
55	        # installer as administrator is disabled by default") unless -RunAsAdmin is
56	        # passed. Some dev boxes always open the shell as admin, so detect that and
57	        # forward the flag. We build a scriptblock from the fetched text so we can
58	        # pass args -- equivalent to `iex "& {$(irm get.scoop.sh)} -RunAsAdmin"`.
59	        $installer = [scriptblock]::Create((Invoke-RestMethod get.scoop.sh))
60	        if (Test-Admin) {
61	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (elevated console: passing -RunAsAdmin)'
62	            & $installer -RunAsAdmin
63	        } else {
64	            Info 'scoop not found — installing (user-scoped, no admin)'
65	            & $installer
66	        }

73	function Get-ScoopInstalled {
74	    try { return @((scoop export | ConvertFrom-Json).apps.name) } catch { return @() }
75	}
76	
77	function Scoop-Install([string[]]$apps) {
78	    if (-not $apps) { return }
79	    # Wrap in @() so a lone survivor stays an array. Without it PowerShell
80	    # unwraps a single match to a bare string, and `scoop install @missing`
81	    # then splats it one CHARACTER per arg => "Couldn't find manifest for 'm'"
82	    # when only "mise" was missing.
83	    $missing = @($apps | Where-Object { ($_ -split '/')[-1] -notin (Get-ScoopInstalled) })
84	    if (-not $missing) { Info 'scoop: all requested tools already present'; return }
85	    Info "scoop install: $($missing -join ', ')"
86	    try { scoop install @missing } catch { Write-Warning "scoop batch error: $_" }
87	    # Anything still absent after the batch = failed (scoop keeps going past a bad one).
88	    $after = Get-ScoopInstalled
89	    foreach ($app in $missing) {
90	        $name = ($app -split '/')[-1]
91	        if ($name -notin $after) { Register-Failure "scoop:$name" }
92	    }
93	}
94	
95	# --- winget ----------------------------------------------------------------
96	function Winget-Install([string]$id) {
97	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "winget:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
98	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
99	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
100	    Info "winget install $id"
101	    winget install --id $id -e --silent --scope user `
102	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
103	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) {
104	        # some packages don't support --scope user; retry machine scope.
105	        winget install --id $id -e --silent `
106	            --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
107	    }
108	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "winget:$id" }
109	}
110	
111	# Microsoft Store packages (installed by Store product id via the msstore
112	# source) — used for apps with no community winget manifest, e.g. Raycast and the
113	# official ChatGPT desktop app. msstore ignores --scope, so we don't pass it.
114	function Winget-InstallStore([string]$id) {
115	    if (-not (Have winget)) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id (winget unavailable)"; return }
116	    winget list --id $id -e --accept-source-agreements *> $null
117	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { return }   # already installed
118	    Info "winget (msstore) install $id"
119	    winget install --id $id -e --source msstore --silent `
120	        --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements 2>&1 | Out-Host
121	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure "msstore:$id" }
122	}

125	function Npm-InstallGlobal([string[]]$pkgs) {

127	    foreach ($p in $pkgs) {
128	        Info "npm i -g $p"
129	        npm install -g $p 2>&1 | Out-Host

132	}

156	# arch for nvim. Add an amd64 gcc that matches the emulated nvim.
157	if ([System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeInformation]::OSArchitecture -eq 'Arm64') {
158	    Info 'ARM64 host: adding amd64 gcc for nvim-treesitter'
159	    Scoop-Install @('gcc')
160	}
161	
162	# gh-dash: GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (`gh dash`). It's a gh extension, not a
163	# scoop package, so it needs gh to be authenticated -- this no-ops on a fresh
164	# machine until `gh auth login`. run_onchange only re-fires when this script
165	# changes, so after authenticating, install by hand (`gh extension install
166	# dlvhdr/gh-dash`) or force a reapply (`chezmoi state delete-bucket
167	# --bucket=entryState`). gh-notify / gh-select from the Unix repo are bash-only
168	# extensions and are intentionally skipped on Windows.
169	if (Have gh) {
170	    gh auth status *> $null
171	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
172	        $ghExts = gh extension list 2>$null | Out-String
173	        if ($ghExts -notmatch 'dlvhdr/gh-dash') {
174	            Info 'gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash'
175	            gh extension install dlvhdr/gh-dash
176	            if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'gh-ext:gh-dash' }
177	        }
178	    } else {
179	        Info 'gh not authenticated -- skipping gh-dash (gh auth login, then reapply)'
180	    }
181	}
182	
183	# node/bun come from scoop above (on ~/scoop/shims, reliably on PATH). We do NOT
184	# use mise here -- see the runtimes note in the core scoop list.
185	
186	# Default Python, uv-managed. --default also creates unversioned python/python3
187	# (not just python3.x) in UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR (~/.local/bin, on PATH via 00_env
188	# ahead of the Store's python.exe app-alias). On uv 0.8+ `--default` lives behind
189	# preview mode, so it must be paired with `--preview` (older uv just ignored it) —
190	# without it, current uv errors: "`--default` is only available in preview mode".
191	if (Have uv) {
192	    Info 'uv python install --default --preview'
193	    $env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local\bin'
194	    uv python install --default --preview 2>&1 | Out-Host
195	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:python' }
196	}
197	
198	# Make PSGallery usable non-interactively before Install-Module (NuGet provider
199	# + trust) so the fzf key bindings / audio cmdlets install reliably.
200	try {
201	    if (-not (Get-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -ListAvailable -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) {
202	        Install-PackageProvider -Name NuGet -Scope CurrentUser -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null
203	    }
204	    if ((Get-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).InstallationPolicy -ne 'Trusted') {
205	        Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
206	    }
207	} catch { Write-Warning "PSGallery prep failed (non-fatal): $_" }
208	
209	# PSReadLine ships with pwsh; PSFzf powers the fzf key bindings in profile.d
210	# (Ctrl+t = files, Ctrl+r = fuzzy history search).
211	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name PSFzf)) {
212	    Info 'Install-Module PSFzf'
213	    Install-Module PSFzf -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
214	}
215	# AudioDeviceCmdlets: absolute volume + explicit mute for the sys* helpers.
216	if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name AudioDeviceCmdlets)) {
217	    Info 'Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets'
218	    Install-Module AudioDeviceCmdlets -Scope CurrentUser -Force -AcceptLicense -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
219	}
220	
221	{{ if .installExtraRuntimes -}}
222	# Extra language runtimes, native via scoop (rust via rustup). node/bun/uv are
223	# baseline; go/rust/ruby added here.
224	Scoop-Install @('go', 'rustup', 'ruby')
225	{{ end -}}
226	
227	{{ if .installMediaTools -}}
228	Scoop-Install @('ffmpeg', 'imagemagick')
229	{{ end -}}
230	
231	{{ if .installCodingAgents -}}
232	# AI coding agents (npm-based CLIs; Antigravity IDE goes via winget below).
233	Npm-InstallGlobal @(
234	    '@anthropic-ai/claude-code',
235	    'opencode-ai',
236	    '@openai/codex',
237	    '@github/copilot'
238	)
239	# NOTE: SpecStory ships no native-Windows CLI (macOS: brew; Linux/WSL: GitHub
240	# release binaries only), so it is intentionally omitted -- `npm i -g
241	# @specstory/cli` 404s. The Copilot module already no-ops when `specstory` is
242	# absent. Experimental Windows build from the unmerged PR #191:
243	# `just specstory-build` (see backlog/specstory-windows-native-cli.md).
244	
245	# OpenCode Desktop (GUI companion to the opencode-ai CLI) via scoop extras.
246	Scoop-Install @('extras/opencode-desktop')
247	{{ end -}}
248	
249	{{ if .installWindowsApps -}}
250	# GUI apps + editors via winget.
251	foreach ($id in @(
252	    'Microsoft.VisualStudioCode',
253	    'Anysphere.Cursor',
254	    'Microsoft.WindowsTerminal',
255	    'Alacritty.Alacritty',
256	    'wez.wezterm',
257	    'Microsoft.PowerToys',
258	    'Google.Antigravity',
259	    'Docker.DockerDesktop',
260	    'Discord.Discord',
261	    # AI desktop app + browsers.
262	    'Anthropic.Claude',
263	    'Google.Chrome',
264	    'TheBrowserCompany.Arc',
265	    'Zen-Team.Zen-Browser'
266	{{- if not .managedMachine }},
267	    'Grammarly.Grammarly'   # skipped on managed machines (often policy-blocked)
268	{{- end }}
269	)) { Winget-Install $id }
270	
271	# Store-only apps: Raycast (default launcher) + the official ChatGPT desktop app.
272	# Neither has a community winget manifest, so they install by Store product id.
273	# Codex is the npm @openai/codex CLI (coding-agents block above), not a Store app.
274	# NB: 9PLM9XGG6VKS is the OpenAI "ChatGPT" listing; the old 9NT1R1C2HH7J was
275	# "ChatGPT Classic", which winget's msstore source no longer resolves ("no
276	# matching package"). Confirm live ids with `winget search --source msstore ...`.
277	Winget-InstallStore '9PFXXSHC64H3'   # Raycast
278	Winget-InstallStore '9PLM9XGG6VKS'   # ChatGPT (OpenAI)
279	
280	# Nerd Fonts so starship glyphs render. Hack-NF-Mono supplies the
281	# "Hack Nerd Font Mono" family the Alacritty / Windows Terminal / editor
282	# configs reference; Hack-NF ("Hack Nerd Font") is the proportional companion.
283	Scoop-Install @('Hack-NF', 'Hack-NF-Mono')
284	
285	Write-Warning 'Raycast is the default launcher but shares Alt+Space with PowerToys Run -- disable PowerToys Run (PowerToys Settings > PowerToys Run > off) or rebind a hotkey. Raycast for Windows is a Store beta; if it fails to install, Flow Launcher is the fallback: winget install Flow-Launcher.Flow-Launcher.'
286	{{ end -}}
287	
288	{{ if .installGamingApps -}}
289	Winget-Install 'Valve.Steam'
290	{{ end -}}
291	
292	{{ if .installUtilityApps -}}
293	# System / utility apps via winget.
294	foreach ($id in @(
295	    'CPUID.CPU-Z',
296	    'TechPowerUp.GPU-Z',
297	    'REALiX.HWiNFO',
298	    'JAMSoftware.TreeSize.Free',
299	    'WinDirStat.WinDirStat',
300	    'voidtools.Everything',
301	    'ShareX.ShareX',
302	    'VideoLAN.VLC',
303	    'OBSProject.OBSStudio',
304	    'Rufus.Rufus'
305	{{- if not .managedMachine }},
306	    'Tailscale.Tailscale'   # skipped on managed machines (org policy blocks the MSI, exit 1625)
307	{{- end }}
308	)) { Winget-Install $id }
309	{{ end -}}
310	
311	{{ if .installLlmTools -}}
312	# Local LLM tools: Ollama (winget) + LiteLLM (uv tool).
313	Winget-Install 'Ollama.Ollama'
314	if (Have uv) { Info 'uv tool install litellm'; uv tool install 'litellm[proxy]'; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'uv:litellm' } }
315	{{ end -}}
316	
317	{{ if .installTunnelTools -}}
318	Scoop-Install @('ngrok', 'cloudflared')
319	{{ end -}}
320	
321	{{ if .installIacTools -}}
322	Scoop-Install @('terraform', 'opentofu')
323	Winget-Install 'Microsoft.AzureCLI'
324	# Azure DevOps CLI (az extension -> `az devops`, `az repos`, `az pipelines`).
325	# It also auto-installs on first `az devops`/`az repos` use; pre-adding here just
326	# skips the first-use prompt. Skipped when az isn't on PATH yet (a fresh winget
327	# install needs a new shell) -- harmless, since first use self-installs it.
328	if (Have az) {
329	    Info 'az extension add azure-devops'
330	    az extension add --name azure-devops --upgrade --only-show-errors
331	    if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { Register-Failure 'az-ext:azure-devops' }
332	}
333	{{ end -}}
334	
335	if ($script:Failures.Count -gt 0) {
336	    Write-Warning ("package install: {0} item(s) failed and were skipped:" -f $script:Failures.Count)
337	    $script:Failures | ForEach-Object { Write-Warning "  - $_" }
338	    Write-Warning "Re-run 'just apply' (or 'chezmoi apply') to retry, or install them manually."
339	} else {
340	    Info 'package install complete — no failures'
341	}
342
```

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/35_yazi.ps1`

```text
1	# 35_yazi.ps1 — `y` launches yazi and cd's to wherever you quit (mirrors the
2	# unix y() wrapper). No-op if yazi isn't installed.
3	if (Get-Command yazi -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
4	    function y {
5	        $tmp = New-TemporaryFile
6	        try {
7	            yazi @args --cwd-file $tmp.FullName
8	            $cwd = (Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim()
9	            if ($cwd -and $cwd -ne $PWD.Path -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cwd)) {
10	                Set-Location -LiteralPath $cwd
11	            }
12	        } finally {
13	            Remove-Item -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
14	        }
15	    }
16	}
17
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`

```text
1	# 00_env.ps1 — environment variables & PATH. Sourced first by $PROFILE.
2	
3	# UTF-8 console I/O. Without this, native tools that emit UTF-8 (git, winget's
4	# localized output, …) render as mojibake on legacy-codepage systems (e.g. GBK /
5	# cp936 on zh-* Windows). Guarded so a redirected/headless host can't error the
6	# rest of this fragment.
7	try {
8	    $utf8 = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
9	    [Console]::OutputEncoding = $utf8
10	    $OutputEncoding = $utf8
11	} catch { $null = $_ }
12	
13	# XDG-style base dirs. Several tools honor these on Windows (starship, atuin,
14	# zoxide state, our own copilot module) so we set them for a tidy $HOME.
15	if (-not $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME) { $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $HOME '.config' }
16	if (-not $env:XDG_DATA_HOME)   { $env:XDG_DATA_HOME   = Join-Path $HOME '.local/share' }
17	if (-not $env:XDG_STATE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_STATE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.local/state' }
18	if (-not $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.cache' }
19	
20	$env:STARSHIP_CONFIG = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'starship.toml'
21	# yazi looks in %APPDATA%\yazi\config by default; keep it XDG-consistent instead.
22	$env:YAZI_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'yazi'
23	
24	# uv-managed Python: `uv python install --default` drops python/python3 here.
25	# ~/.local/bin is prepended to PATH below, so they win over the Windows Store
26	# `python.exe` app-execution-alias.
27	$env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'
28	
29	# Default editor
30	if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $env:EDITOR = 'nvim' }
31	
32	# Prepend user bin dirs to PATH, idempotently. scoop already adds its shims to
33	# the persisted user PATH, but a fresh session inside chezmoi apply may not have
34	# it yet; ~/.local/bin carries uv tools + the uv-managed python. Normalize each
35	# entry to backslashes (mixed slashes can fail Windows command resolution).
36	$UserPaths = @(
37	    (Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'),
38	    (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/shims')
39	) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } |
40	    ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($_) } | Select-Object -Unique
41	
42	$SepPaths = $env:PATH -split ';'
43	foreach ($p in $UserPaths) {
44	    if ($SepPaths -notcontains $p) { $env:PATH = "$p;$env:PATH" }
45	}
46
```
</details></tool-use>

1	# ~/.config/yazi/yazi.toml (Windows). Resolved via $YAZI_CONFIG_HOME, set in
2	# profile.d/00_env.ps1. Adapted from the macOS/Linux dotfiles: the layout/sort
3	# settings port verbatim; openers use Windows-native commands.
4	#
5	# yazi 25/26 uses the [mgr] table (older versions used [manager]).
6	
7	[mgr]
8	ratio = [1, 4, 3]          # 1/8 parent, 4/8 current, 3/8 preview
9	sort_by = "natural"
10	sort_dir_first = true
11	linemode = "size"
12	show_hidden = true
13	show_symlink = true
14	
15	[open]
16	rules = [
17	  # edit / open / reveal come from yazi's built-in Windows preset; `git` is ours.
18	  { url = "*/", use = ["edit", "git", "reveal", "open"] },
19	  { mime = "text/*", use = ["edit", "open"] },
20	  { mime = "*", use = ["open"] },
21	]
22	
23	[opener]
24	# Open lazygit in the hovered directory. %* is the selected path on Windows;
25	# adjust the placeholder if your yazi version differs.
26	git = [
27	  { run = 'cmd /c cd /d "%*" && lazygit', block = true, desc = "Lazygit (here)" },
28	]
29

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_03_xdg_env.ps1`

```text
1	#Requires -Version 7
2	# run_onchange_after_03_xdg_env.ps1 — persist the XDG base-dir env vars to the
3	# User environment so ~/.config is authoritative for XDG-aware tools (Neovim,
4	# lazygit, starship, yazi, atuin, zoxide) even when launched OUTSIDE a pwsh
5	# session that sourced our profile.
6	#
7	# Why this matters on Windows:
8	#   * 00_env.ps1 sets these in-session, so tools launched from pwsh already use
9	#     ~/.config. But nvim launched from Explorer / a non-pwsh terminal would
10	#     fall back to %LOCALAPPDATA%\nvim for config AND a separate data/plugin
11	#     dir — a confusing "my plugins vanished" split.
12	#   * Setting XDG_CONFIG_HOME is also the Neovim-documented way to make it read
13	#     ~/.config/nvim on Windows, and lazygit honours it for config.yml too.
14	#
15	# Idempotent + fault-tolerant: only writes a var when it differs; never aborts.
16	$ErrorActionPreference = 'Continue'
17	
18	$want = @{
19	    XDG_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $HOME '.config'
20	    XDG_DATA_HOME   = Join-Path $HOME '.local\share'
21	    XDG_STATE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.local\state'
22	    XDG_CACHE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.cache'
23	}
24	
25	foreach ($name in $want.Keys) {
26	    try {
27	        $current = [System.Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable($name, 'User')
28	        if ($current -ne $want[$name]) {
29	            [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable($name, $want[$name], 'User')
30	            Write-Host "==> set User env $name = $($want[$name])" -ForegroundColor Cyan
31	        }
32	    } catch {
33	        Write-Warning "could not set $name (non-fatal): $_"
34	    }
35	}
36
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 23:35:00Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/tools.md`

```text
1	# Tools
2	
3	The install set lives in `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`
4	(the single source of truth), gated by the init toggles. **scoop** handles CLI
5	tools; **winget** handles GUI apps.
6	
7	## CLI tools (scoop)
8	
9	| Tool | Purpose |
10	|---|---|
11	| git, gh, glab | version control + GitHub / GitLab CLIs |
12	| neovim | editor (LazyVim, pre-configured) |
13	| lazygit | git TUI (delta pager configured) |
14	| zoxide | smarter `cd` |
15	| fzf, fd, ripgrep | fuzzy find / file search / grep |
16	| bat, eza | `cat` / `ls` replacements |
17	| delta | git diff pager |
18	| jq | JSON processor |
19	| yazi, btop | file manager / system monitor |
20	| television (`tv`) | fuzzy picker / channel launcher |
21	| tldr (tlrc) | community man-page cheatsheets; `tldrf` adds `zh_TW → zh → en` fallback |
22	| gh dash | GitHub PR/issue dashboard TUI (gh extension; installs once `gh` is authenticated) |
23	| starship | prompt |
24	| node | JS runtime (`nodejs-lts`) |
25	| uv | Python package/runtime manager |
26	| bun | JS runtime + package manager (backs copilot-proxy) |
27	| just | task runner (this repo's `justfile`) |
28	| make | GNU make |
29	| zig | C compiler for Neovim tree-sitter parsers |
30	| gcc | amd64 C compiler (**ARM64 hosts only** — matches the x64-emulated nvim) |
31	| tree-sitter | tree-sitter CLI (nvim-treesitter `main` branch) |
32	| win32yank | clipboard provider for Neovim |
33	| gnupg | `gpg` (git commit signing, verification) |
34	
35	Runtimes are **native (scoop), not mise** — see [rationale](rationale.md#runtimes-scoop-natives-not-mise-on-windows).
36	`node` (`nodejs-lts`) and `bun` come from scoop; `go`, `rust` (rustup), `ruby`
37	are added when **Extra runtimes** is enabled. A default **Python** is uv-managed:
38	`uv python install --default --preview` puts `python`/`python3` in `~/.local/bin`
39	(on PATH ahead of the Store's `python.exe` app-alias), so `python`, `uv run`, and
40	`uv venv` all work without the Microsoft Store.
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/10_tools.ps1`

```text
1	# 10_tools.ps1 — activate CLI tools. Each block no-ops if the tool is absent,
2	# so a partial install (or the minimal role) never errors the prompt.
3	#
4	# Tool `init` output (starship/zoxide/atuin/direnv/tv) is cached under
5	# $XDG_CACHE_HOME/pwsh-init so a warm shell dot-sources a static file instead of
6	# cold-spawning the binary on every start — the dominant pwsh-startup cost on a
7	# fresh box (each spawn is a process launch + Defender scan). The cache
8	# auto-regenerates when the tool's exe timestamp changes (e.g. a scoop upgrade);
9	# delete ~/.cache/pwsh-init to force a rebuild.
10	function Import-CachedInit {
11	    param(
12	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Name,
13	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Exe,
14	        [Parameter(Mandatory)][scriptblock]$Generate
15	    )
16	    $cmd = Get-Command $Exe -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
17	    if (-not $cmd) { return }
18	    $cacheDir  = Join-Path $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME 'pwsh-init'
19	    $cacheFile = Join-Path $cacheDir "$Name.ps1"
20	    $stampFile = "$cacheFile.stamp"
21	    try { $stamp = (Get-Item -LiteralPath $cmd.Source).LastWriteTimeUtc.Ticks.ToString() } catch { $stamp = '' }
22	    $fresh = (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheFile) -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $stampFile) -and
23	             ((Get-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Raw -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim() -eq $stamp)
24	    if (-not $fresh) {
25	        try {
26	            $out = & $Generate | Out-String
27	            if (-not $out.Trim()) { return }   # tool errored / empty output — retry next start
28	            if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cacheDir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $cacheDir | Out-Null }
29	            Set-Content -LiteralPath $cacheFile -Value $out   -Encoding utf8
30	            Set-Content -LiteralPath $stampFile -Value $stamp -Encoding utf8
31	        } catch { return }
32	    }
33	    try { . $cacheFile } catch { Write-Warning "profile: cached init '$Name' failed: $_" }
34	}
35	
36	# starship prompt
37	Import-CachedInit -Name 'starship' -Exe 'starship' -Generate { starship init powershell }
38	
39	# zoxide — smarter cd (rebinds `cd` to `z`)
40	Import-CachedInit -Name 'zoxide' -Exe 'zoxide' -Generate { zoxide init powershell --cmd cd }
41	
42	# Runtimes (node/bun/go/rust/ruby) are native via scoop and Python via uv — no
43	# mise on Windows (its activate/shims are unreliable here). See docs/rationale.
44	
45	# atuin — SQLite shell history with fuzzy search (Ctrl+R)
46	Import-CachedInit -Name 'atuin' -Exe 'atuin' -Generate { atuin init powershell }
47	
48	# fzf — fuzzy finder + PSFzf key bindings (Ctrl+t files, Ctrl+r history)
49	if (Get-Command fzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
50	    $env:FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS = '--height 40% --layout=reverse --border'
51	    if (Get-Command fd -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
52	        $env:FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND = 'fd --hidden --strip-cwd-prefix --exclude .git'
53	    }
54	    # Import directly — a `Get-Module -ListAvailable` guard scans all of
55	    # PSModulePath (slow, esp. under OneDrive-hydrated Documents\PowerShell\Modules).
56	    # `Get-Module -Name` checks only loaded modules, so it's cheap.
57	    Import-Module PSFzf -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
58	    if (Get-Module -Name PSFzf) {
59	        Set-PsFzfOption -PSReadlineChordProvider 'Ctrl+t' -PSReadlineChordReverseHistory 'Ctrl+r'
60	    }
61	}
62	
63	# direnv — per-directory environments
64	Import-CachedInit -Name 'direnv' -Exe 'direnv' -Generate { direnv hook pwsh }
65	
66	# television (tv) — fuzzy picker shell integration. tv 0.15+ renamed the shell
67	# value `powershell` -> `power-shell`; older builds used `powershell`. Try the
68	# new spelling first, fall back, and only cache non-empty output so a version
69	# mismatch never errors the prompt.
70	Import-CachedInit -Name 'tv' -Exe 'tv' -Generate {
71	    $o = tv init power-shell 2>$null | Out-String
72	    if (-not $o.Trim()) { $o = tv init powershell 2>$null | Out-String }
73	    $o
74	}
75
```
</details></tool-use>

## 1. How yazi is installed

yazi is installed via **scoop**, in the "Core CLI toolset — the shell baseline, always installed" block (lines 137–152). There is **no install toggle** — yazi is unconditional (it's in the always-installed baseline, not gated behind any `installYazi`/`if` block):

```powershell
# Core CLI toolset — the shell baseline, always installed.
Scoop-Install @(
    'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', 'make', 'just',
    'starship', 'zoxide', 'fzf', 'fd', 'ripgrep', 'bat', 'eza', 'jq',

`Scoop-Install` (lines 77–93) diffs against `scoop export` and only installs missing apps. Not npm, not winget.

## 2. How Git for Windows is installed

**File:** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`, line 139 (same core scoop list as yazi):

```powershell
Scoop-Install @(
    'git', 'gh', 'glab', '7zip', ...
```

Git is installed via **scoop**, not winget. This is significant for your fix: scoop's git lives at `~/scoop/apps/git/current/`, so `file.exe` is at **`~/scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe`** — NOT the winget `C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe` path you mentioned. (There is no winget `Git.Git` line anywhere in the script.) Note scoop typically also exposes a `current` symlink and shims under `~/scoop/shims`, but the MSYS `file.exe` is under `usr/bin`, which scoop does not shim.

## 3. The yazi PowerShell profile fragment

**File:** `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/35_yazi.ps1` (full contents):

```powershell
# 35_yazi.ps1 — `y` launches yazi and cd's to wherever you quit (mirrors the
# unix y() wrapper). No-op if yazi isn't installed.
if (Get-Command yazi -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
    function y {
        $tmp = New-TemporaryFile
        try {
            yazi @args --cwd-file $tmp.FullName
            $cwd = (Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Trim()
            if ($cwd -and $cwd -ne $PWD.Path -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $cwd)) {
                Set-Location -LiteralPath $cwd
            }
        } finally {
            Remove-Item -LiteralPath $tmp.FullName -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        }
    }
}
```

This fragment sets **no env vars** — it only defines the `y` shell-wrapper function (guarded by a `Get-Command yazi` presence check). `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` is NOT set here; it's set in `00_env.ps1` (see point 4). There is no `YAZI_FILE_ONE` here.

## 4. The env / environment-variable profile fragment

**File:** `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1` (full contents):

```powershell
# 00_env.ps1 — environment variables & PATH. Sourced first by $PROFILE.

# UTF-8 console I/O. ...
try {
    $utf8 = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
    [Console]::OutputEncoding = $utf8
    $OutputEncoding = $utf8
} catch { $null = $_ }

# XDG-style base dirs. ...
if (-not $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME) { $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $HOME '.config' }
if (-not $env:XDG_DATA_HOME)   { $env:XDG_DATA_HOME   = Join-Path $HOME '.local/share' }
if (-not $env:XDG_STATE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_STATE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.local/state' }
if (-not $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.cache' }

$env:STARSHIP_CONFIG = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'starship.toml'
# yazi looks in %APPDATA%\yazi\config by default; keep it XDG-consistent instead.
$env:YAZI_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'yazi'

# uv-managed Python: ...
$env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'

# Default editor
if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $env:EDITOR = 'nvim' }

# Prepend user bin dirs to PATH, idempotently. ...
$UserPaths = @(
    (Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'),
    (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/shims')
) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } |
    ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($_) } | Select-Object -Unique

$SepPaths = $env:PATH -split ';'
foreach ($p in $UserPaths) {
    if ($SepPaths -notcontains $p) { $env:PATH = "$p;$env:PATH" }
}
```

**Idioms used here:**
- Session env vars are set with `$env:FOO = ...`, guarded by `if (-not $env:FOO)` for the XDG dirs (so a pre-existing value wins), and unconditionally for derived ones like `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME`.
- **`YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` is set here** (line 22) to `$env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME/yazi` (= `~/.config/yazi`).
- PATH is prepended idempotently by splitting on `;` and only adding entries not already present.

**Related file — persistent (User-scope) env vars:** `.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_03_xdg_env.ps1`. This is the counterpart that persists the same XDG vars to the **User environment** (so tools launched outside a pwsh session that sourced the profile still honor `~/.config`). It uses the `[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable($name, $value, 'User')` idiom (lines 25–35), writing only when the value differs. Note: it persists only the four `XDG_*` vars — it does NOT persist `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` (that one is session-only via `00_env.ps1`).

# profile.d/00_env.ps1. ...
# yazi 25/26 uses the [mgr] table (older versions used [manager]).

[mgr]
ratio = [1, 4, 3]
sort_by = "natural"
sort_dir_first = true
linemode = "size"
show_hidden = true
show_symlink = true

[open]
rules = [

  { mime = "text/*", use = ["edit", "open"] },

]

[opener]
git = [

]
```

Since Git is installed via scoop, I need to add this configuration to the PowerShell profile environment setup file, pointing to the correct path in the scoop directory. I'm leaning toward keeping it session-scoped to match the existing `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` pattern, even though that means yazi won't have the variable if launched outside pwsh. For the actual path, I need to construct it carefully using `Join-Path` and guard it with `Test-Path` to handle both the scoop installation and potential winget/installer paths.

Setting up the fallback logic... I'm settling on 00_env.ps1 as the right place since YAZI_CONFIG_HOME already sets precedent for yazi environment variables there, and it keeps related config together. Using YAZI_FILE_ONE is cleaner than polluting PATH with MSYS binaries anyway. Now I'm thinking about whether this warrants documentation updates — it's not a new installer tool, but it's a meaningful behavior change worth documenting. eat it as a pitfall worth documenting since the error message is searchable and relevant to this repo's specific setup. For verification, I'd run PSScriptAnalyzer on the changes and check if there are existing Pester tests for profile fragments that I should mirror, though adding a test for the environment variable might be overkill. Now I'm considering whether to also handle cases where `file` isn't available.

If a user already has `file` on their PATH, yazi finds it automatically without needing YAZI_FILE_ONE set. My guard clause only sets it when file.exe exists at a known location, which is safe. I could check `Get-Command file` first to skip if it's already available, but explicitly setting YAZI_FILE_ONE to the Git installation path is harmless even if file is on PATH. The key thing is that MSYS file.exe needs its magic database, which Git for Windows ships alongside the executable, and file.exe locates the magic relative to its own location—so pointing YAZI_FILE_ONE at the full path handles that automatically.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pitfalls/README.md`

```text
1	# Pitfalls
2	
3	Past traps we've stepped on. **Symptoms-first** knowledge base — when a
4	problem recurs (on a new machine, after an upgrade, with a new tool combo),
5	grepping the symptom here lands you on the root cause and workaround in
6	seconds, instead of re-debugging from scratch.
7	
8	This folder is excluded from <DEPLOYMENT/PACKAGING MECHANISM, e.g. chezmoi via
9	.chezmoiignore.tmpl, Python via MANIFEST.in, npm via .npmignore> — it is repo
10	metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
11	
12	## Pitfalls vs the rest
13	
14	| Surface | Time direction | Question it answers | Access pattern |
15	|---|---|---|---|
16	| `docs/<tool>.md` | Present | "How does this tool work / how do I configure it?" | Read top to bottom |
17	| `pitfalls/<slug>.md` | **Past** | **"I see error X — has this happened before?"** | **Grep symptoms** |
18	| `backlog/<slug>.md` | Future | "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" | Index in `TODO.md` |
19	| `AGENTS.md` Hard invariants | Present | "What rules MUST agents follow?" | Read top to bottom |
20	
21	A pitfall **graduates** to a Hard invariant when the trap is serious enough
22	that you can't rely on memory or grep — typically when (a) it recurs across
23	machines, (b) it silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
24	and easy to undo by accident. When graduating, leave a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
25	as historical record and link to it from the new invariant.
26	
27	## When to add a pitfall doc
28	
29	Add `pitfalls/<slug>.md` when you've spent more than ~15 minutes on something
30	that wasn't googleable, AND any of:
31	
32	- The symptom is non-obvious from the root cause (silent state, weird side
33	  effect, behaviour change without error)
34	- The fix is "do nothing different but in a specific order"
35	- The same trap could be hit by a new agent / new machine / new contributor
36	- An upstream bug exists with no ETA — workaround needs to outlive memory
37	- A specific tool version is required (or forbidden) and failure at the
38	  wrong version is silent / confusing
39	
40	## When NOT to add a pitfall doc
41	
42	- Trivially googleable (next person solves in 30 seconds)
43	- Already covered in `docs/<tool>.md` — cross-link from this README's
44	  "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table below instead of duplicating
45	- Already a Hard invariant (cross-link only)
46	- One-off transient (network glitch, machine-specific config rot)
47	
48	## File template
49	
50	See [`pitfall-doc.md.template`] in the
51	[`project-knowledge-harness` skill](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/local/project-knowledge-harness)
52	for the per-doc template.
53	
54	Key sections (different from `backlog/` template — symptom-first, not
55	context-first):
56	
57	```markdown
58	# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>
59	
60	**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
61	**First seen**: YYYY-MM
62	**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
63	**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
64

# Fix yazi "Cannot find `file` to detect the file's MIME type" on Windows

## Context

On Windows, launching yazi errors on every entry with:

\```
Cannot find `file` to detect the file's MIME type. Set it up correctly as per the Windows Installation Guide.
\```

yazi delegates MIME detection to the MSYS `file(1)` command. Unlike macOS/Linux
(where `file` is on PATH), Windows has no `file` unless you point yazi at the one
**bundled with Git for Windows**. yazi's official [Windows install guide](https://yazi-rs.github.io/docs/installation/#windows)
tells you to either add Git's `usr\bin` to PATH or — cleaner — set the
`YAZI_FILE_ONE` environment variable to the full path of `file.exe`.

This repo installs **git via scoop** (`.chezmoiscripts/run_onchange_after_10_packages.ps1.tmpl`
core CLI list), so `file.exe` lives at
`~/scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe` — **not** the generic
`C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe` the yazi docs assume. scoop shims
`~/scoop/shims` onto PATH but does **not** shim MSYS `usr/bin`, so `file.exe` is
invisible to yazi by default. The repo currently sets `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` in
`00_env.ps1` but has **no** `YAZI_FILE_ONE` anywhere (confirmed: 0 hits repo-wide).

Intended outcome: a fresh pwsh session automatically exposes `file.exe` to yazi
so MIME detection works, with no manual step, robust to git being installed via
scoop **or** a system/winget installer.

## The change

### Single file: `dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`

Add a `YAZI_FILE_ONE` block immediately after the existing `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME`
line (line 22). This is the correct home: `00_env.ps1` is the env/PATH fragment,
it already owns the yazi config var, and it loads (order `00`) well before the
`y` wrapper in `35_yazi.ps1` (order `35`). Match the fragment's existing idioms
(`Where-Object { Test-Path … }` + `Select-Object`, as used by the PATH block at
lines 36–40):

\```powershell
# yazi needs the MSYS `file.exe` (bundled with Git) for MIME detection; without
# it every entry errors "Cannot find `file` … Set it up per the Windows guide".
# scoop doesn't shim git's usr/bin, so point YAZI_FILE_ONE at file.exe directly;
# fall back to a system/winget Git install. First existing candidate wins.
if (-not $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE) {
    $fileOne = @(
        (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe'),
        'C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe'
    ) | Where-Object { Test-Path -LiteralPath $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
    if ($fileOne) { $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE = $fileOne }
}
\```

Notes on the design:
- **`if (-not $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE)`** — respect a user override (mirrors the XDG
  guards above) and keep it idempotent across re-sourcing.
- **`if ($fileOne)` guard before assigning** — if no candidate exists, leave the
  var unset. Assigning `$null`/`''` would set `YAZI_FILE_ONE=""`, which is worse
  than unset (yazi would try to exec an empty path).
- **Session-scope `$env:` (not User-scope persistence)** — deliberately
  consistent with `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME`, which is also session-only. yazi's intended
  entry point here is the `y` wrapper (`35_yazi.ps1`), always inside a
  profile-sourced pwsh; a yazi launched outside pwsh wouldn't find its config
  either, so persisting only `YAZI_FILE_ONE` would be half a solution. No change
  to `run_onchange_after_03_xdg_env.ps1`.
- **Forward-slash path in `Join-Path`** — matches the existing `scoop/shims`
  usage in this same file; `Join-Path`/`Test-Path` normalize separators.

## Out of scope (with reasons)

- **No packages-script change** — `file.exe` ships with git, which is already in
  the always-installed core scoop list. Nothing new to install.
- **No docs mirror** — yazi already has its row in `docs/tools.md` /
  `docs/tools.zh-TW.md`; this adds no new installer tool, prompt, or docs page,
  so the CLAUDE.md cross-file-mirror invariants don't trigger.
- **No `pitfalls/` doc** — per `pitfalls/README.md` "When NOT to add": the symptom
  is documented in yazi's own install guide (googleable) and, once this lands, the
  profile fixes it automatically so it can't recur on a configured box.
- **No Pester test** — asserting the var would require `file.exe` present on the
  CI host; PSScriptAnalyzer (`-Recurse`, which lints this non-`.tmpl` file) already
  covers syntax, and this is pure env plumbing.

## Verification

Off-Windows (this checkout — logic + syntax, no real `file.exe`):

1. **Lint** (must report no new Errors):
   \```bash
   pwsh -NoProfile -c "Invoke-ScriptAnalyzer -Path ./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1 -Settings ./PSScriptAnalyzerSettings.psd1"
   \```
2. **Parse** the fragment:
   \```bash
   pwsh -NoProfile -c "\$e=\$null; [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile((Resolve-Path ./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1), [ref]\$null, [ref]\$e); if (\$e){throw \$e}"
   \```
3. **Logic smoke test** — stub the scoop path under a throwaway `$HOME` and
   dot-source, asserting `YAZI_FILE_ONE` resolves to the stub (and that a missing
   file leaves it unset):
   \```bash
   pwsh -NoProfile -c '
     $HOME = New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path (Join-Path ([IO.Path]::GetTempPath()) ([guid]::NewGuid())) -Force
     New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path (Join-Path $HOME "scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin") | Out-Null
     Set-Content (Join-Path $HOME "scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe") "stub"
     . ./dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1
     if (-not $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE) { throw "not set" }
     "OK -> $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE"'
   \```

On the real Windows box (the actual gate):

4. `chezmoi apply` (or `just apply`), open a **fresh** pwsh, confirm
   `echo $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE` prints the scoop `file.exe` path.
5. Run `y` (the yazi wrapper); browse a folder with mixed file types — the
   "Cannot find `file`…" banner is gone and previews/MIME-based open rules work.
6. `windows-latest` CI (`.github/workflows/windows.yml`) stays green
   (PSScriptAnalyzer + init/apply).

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 23:39:23Z)**_

I wasn't able to load `ExitPlanMode` (tool search is returning "not enabled" in this session), so I'll present the plan here directly. The plan file is written to `.claude/plans/windows-yazi-cannot-proud-frost.md`.

## Diagnosis

yazi delegates MIME detection to the MSYS `file(1)` command. On Windows that only exists bundled with Git for Windows. Your repo installs **git via scoop**, so `file.exe` sits at `~/scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe` — and scoop shims `~/scoop/shims` but **not** MSYS `usr/bin`, so `file.exe` is invisible to yazi. The repo sets `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` but never sets its sibling `YAZI_FILE_ONE` (0 hits repo-wide), which is exactly what yazi's Windows guide says to point at `file.exe`.

Note: the path in the docs you quoted (`C:\Program Files\Git\...`) is the *installer* layout; scoop's is different — the fix probes both.

## The fix (one file)

`dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1`, right after the `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` line, matching the fragment's existing `Where-Object {Test-Path} | Select-Object` idiom:

```powershell
# yazi needs the MSYS `file.exe` (bundled with Git) for MIME detection; without
# it every entry errors "Cannot find `file` … Set it up per the Windows guide".
# scoop doesn't shim git's usr/bin, so point YAZI_FILE_ONE at file.exe directly;
# fall back to a system/winget Git install. First existing candidate wins.
if (-not $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE) {
    $fileOne = @(
        (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe'),
        'C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe'
    ) | Where-Object { Test-Path -LiteralPath $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
    if ($fileOne) { $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE = $fileOne }
}
```

Key choices: user-override guard, only assign when a candidate exists (empty path would be worse than unset), and **session-scope** to stay consistent with `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME` (yazi's entry here is the `y` wrapper, always inside a profile-sourced pwsh).

**Deliberately out of scope:** no packages-script change (file.exe ships with already-installed git), no docs/tools mirror (no new tool), no pitfalls doc (yazi documents this; the profile fix makes it non-recurring), no Pester test (pure env plumbing; PSScriptAnalyzer covers syntax).

**Verification:** lint + parse + a `$HOME`-stub logic smoke test off-Windows; then on the real box `chezmoi apply`, confirm `$env:YAZI_FILE_ONE` is populated, and run `y` to confirm the banner is gone (full commands in the plan file).

## One decision for you

The plan is scoped to the minimal fix above. Before I implement — do you want the env var **session-only** (my recommendation, consistent with `YAZI_CONFIG_HOME`) or also **persisted to the User environment** so a yazi launched outside a pwsh session (e.g. double-clicked / from Windows Terminal without the profile) also gets it? The latter adds a line to `run_onchange_after_03_xdg_env.ps1`, but note config wouldn't be found in that scenario anyway.

If you're happy with session-only as planned, just say "go" and I'll implement it on this worktree branch and open a draft PR.

1	# 00_env.ps1 — environment variables & PATH. Sourced first by $PROFILE.
2	
3	# UTF-8 console I/O. Without this, native tools that emit UTF-8 (git, winget's
4	# localized output, …) render as mojibake on legacy-codepage systems (e.g. GBK /
5	# cp936 on zh-* Windows). Guarded so a redirected/headless host can't error the
6	# rest of this fragment.
7	try {
8	    $utf8 = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
9	    [Console]::OutputEncoding = $utf8
10	    $OutputEncoding = $utf8
11	} catch { $null = $_ }
12	
13	# XDG-style base dirs. Several tools honor these on Windows (starship, atuin,
14	# zoxide state, our own copilot module) so we set them for a tidy $HOME.
15	if (-not $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME) { $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $HOME '.config' }
16	if (-not $env:XDG_DATA_HOME)   { $env:XDG_DATA_HOME   = Join-Path $HOME '.local/share' }
17	if (-not $env:XDG_STATE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_STATE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.local/state' }
18	if (-not $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME)  { $env:XDG_CACHE_HOME  = Join-Path $HOME '.cache' }
19	
20	$env:STARSHIP_CONFIG = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'starship.toml'
21	# yazi looks in %APPDATA%\yazi\config by default; keep it XDG-consistent instead.
22	$env:YAZI_CONFIG_HOME = Join-Path $env:XDG_CONFIG_HOME 'yazi'
23	
24	# uv-managed Python: `uv python install --default` drops python/python3 here.
25	# ~/.local/bin is prepended to PATH below, so they win over the Windows Store
26	# `python.exe` app-execution-alias.
27	$env:UV_PYTHON_BIN_DIR = Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'
28	
29	# Default editor
30	if (Get-Command nvim -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) { $env:EDITOR = 'nvim' }
31	
32	# Prepend user bin dirs to PATH, idempotently. scoop already adds its shims to
33	# the persisted user PATH, but a fresh session inside chezmoi apply may not have
34	# it yet; ~/.local/bin carries uv tools + the uv-managed python. Normalize each
35	# entry to backslashes (mixed slashes can fail Windows command resolution).
36	$UserPaths = @(
37	    (Join-Path $HOME '.local/bin'),
38	    (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/shims')
39	) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } |
40	    ForEach-Object { [System.IO.Path]::GetFullPath($_) } | Select-Object -Unique
41	
42	$SepPaths = $env:PATH -split ';'
43	foreach ($p in $UserPaths) {
44	    if ($SepPaths -notcontains $p) { $env:PATH = "$p;$env:PATH" }
45	}
46

+    $fileOne = @(
+        (Join-Path $HOME 'scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe'),
+        'C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe'
+    ) | Where-Object { Test-Path -LiteralPath $_ } | Select-Object -First 1
+    if ($fileOne) { $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE = $fileOne }
+}
+
 # uv-managed Python: `uv python install --default` drops python/python3 here.
 # ~/.local/bin is prepended to PATH below, so they win over the Windows Store
 # `python.exe` app-execution-alias.
=== is .claude/plans tracked/ignored? ===
NOT ignored
```

## Problem

On Windows, yazi errors on every entry with:

> Cannot find \`file\` to detect the file's MIME type. Set it up correctly as per the Windows Installation Guide.

yazi delegates MIME detection to the MSYS \`file(1)\` command, which on Windows only exists **bundled with Git**. This repo installs git via **scoop**, so \`file.exe\` lives at \`~/scoop/apps/git/current/usr/bin/file.exe\` — and scoop shims \`~/scoop/shims\` but **not** MSYS \`usr/bin\`, so \`file.exe\` is invisible to yazi by default. The repo already sets \`YAZI_CONFIG_HOME\` but never its sibling \`YAZI_FILE_ONE\`, which is what yazi's [Windows install guide](https://yazi-rs.github.io/docs/installation/#windows) says to point at \`file.exe\`.

## Change

One block added to \`dot_config/powershell/profile.d/00_env.ps1\`, right after \`YAZI_CONFIG_HOME\`, matching the fragment's existing \`Where-Object { Test-Path } | Select-Object\` idiom:

- Probes the scoop git path first, then a system/winget \`C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin\file.exe\` fallback — first existing candidate wins.
- \`if (-not $env:YAZI_FILE_ONE)\` respects a user override and stays idempotent.
- Only assigns when a candidate exists (an empty \`YAZI_FILE_ONE\` would be worse than unset).
- **Session-scope**, deliberately consistent with the neighbouring session-only \`YAZI_CONFIG_HOME\`.

## Verification (off-Windows)

- PSScriptAnalyzer: clean (no findings)
- Parse: OK

  - stub \`file.exe\` present → \`YAZI_FILE_ONE\` resolves to it
  - absent → left **unset** (not empty string)
  - pre-set override → preserved

On a real Windows box: \`chezmoi apply\`, open a fresh pwsh, \`$env:YAZI_FILE_ONE\` is populated, and \`y\` no longer shows the "Cannot find \`file\`" banner. \`windows-latest\` CI is the real gate.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

Add .claude/worktrees/ to .gitignore

```bash

python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p = Path(".gitignore")
lines = p.read_text().splitlines()
entry = ".claude/worktrees/"
if entry in lines:
    print("already present, no change")
else:
    out = []
    inserted = False
    for ln in lines:
        out.append(ln)
        # insert right after the commented '# .claude/' note so it sits in the
        # agent/session artifacts section
        if ln.strip() == "# .claude/" and not inserted:
            out.append(".claude/worktrees/   # ephemeral EnterWorktree checkouts; never commit")
            inserted = True
    if not inserted:  # fallback: append
        out.append(entry)
    p.write_text("\n".join(out) + "\n")
    print("inserted")
PY
echo "=== resulting .gitignore ==="; cat -n .gitignore
echo "=== confirm now ignored ==="; git check-ignore .claude/worktrees/ && echo IGNORED || echo "NOT ignored"

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is that `git check-ignore` returns a non-zero exit code for `.claude/worktrees/` even though the pattern exists in the gitignore file. This happens because the directory doesn't actually exist on disk—ExitWorktree removed it—and while `git check-ignore` can evaluate patterns regardless of whether paths exist, there's something about how it's handling this particular case that's causing it to report the path as not ignored.

Wait, I see the real problem now: the inline comment! In gitignore files, a `#` only starts a comment when it appears at the beginning of a line. A trailing `# ...` on the same line as a pattern isn't treated as a comment—it becomes part of the pattern itself. So the line `.claude/worktrees/   # ephemeral...` is being interpreted as a literal pattern that includes all those trailing spaces and characters, which obviously doesn't match the actual path `.claude/worktrees/`. That's why check-ignore says it's not ignored.

Gitignore doesn't support inline comments at all, so I need to move the explanation to its own line above the pattern. I'll also need to be careful about trailing whitespace since gitignore treats that as significant. The fix is to create a two-line block with the comment on top and the clean pattern below it.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 23:57:14Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-10 23:57:15Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Fix gitignore to own-line comment and verify

```bash
cd /Users/david/src/tries/2026-07-09-windows-dotfiles
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
p = Path(".gitignore")
lines = p.read_text().splitlines()
# drop the malformed inline-comment line
lines = [ln for ln in lines if not ln.startswith(".claude/worktrees/")]
out, inserted = [], False
for ln in lines:
    out.append(ln)
    if ln.strip() == "# .claude/" and not inserted:
        out.append("# ephemeral EnterWorktree checkouts; never commit")
        out.append(".claude/worktrees/")
        inserted = True
if not inserted:
    out += ["# ephemeral EnterWorktree checkouts; never commit", ".claude/worktrees/"]
p.write_text("\n".join(out) + "\n")
print("rewritten")
PY
echo "=== .gitignore (agent section) ==="; sed -n '17,22p' .gitignore
echo "=== confirm now ignored (create a probe path) ==="
mkdir -p .claude/worktrees/_probe
git check-ignore -v .claude/worktrees/_probe && echo IGNORED || echo "NOT ignored"
rmdir .claude/worktrees/_probe .claude/worktrees 2>/dev/null; true
```
